steps.title: Identify the subject of the verb
steps.content: The blank follows “which” and refers to “Chicago Midway International Airport,” a singular noun. Therefore the verb must agree with a singular subject.

steps.title: Apply subject‑verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: a singular subject takes a singular present-tense verb.
  • Choice A — "is": singular present-tense verb; agrees with singular "Chicago Midway International Airport." ✓
  • Choice B — "have been": plural present-perfect verb; disagrees with a singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — "were": plural past-tense verb; disagrees with a singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — "are": plural present-tense verb; disagrees with a singular subject. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
